title: T-088 — UI: TEC-045 Lab Results Log spec-driven screen
prompt:
# T-088 — UI: TEC-045 Lab Results Log (spec-driven)

Implement the MVP UI task for TEC-045 Lab Results Log. Exact canonical prototype is missing, so this is a spec-driven UI task: `docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md` §10.7 / Quality-owned lab read-model log and `prototypes/design/03-TECHNICAL-UX.md` are authoritative; adjacent Technical prototype elements may be reused only as layout/primitives, not as a false 1:1 source. Route target: `/technical/lab-results`. Apply 03-TECHNICAL red-lines (FG/WIP, factory_specs, shared BOM SSOT, Quality lab ownership, D365 optional) where applicable. Closeout requires screenshot/artifacts plus Playwright traces/artifacts per shared UI policy.

pipeline_inputs.description: UI: TEC-045 Lab Results Log spec-driven screen
pipeline_inputs.details:
Spec-driven because only a per-item lab tab exists. Implement cross-item lab-results log as a Technical read model over Quality-owned lab_results. Filters: item, WO, test type, result status. Add/request flow must delegate through Quality bridge or show QUALITY_BRIDGE_MISSING; Technical must not write lab_results directly.

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- Given closeout is prepared, screenshots/artifacts and Playwright traces/artifacts where applicable are listed per shared UI policy.
- Given user adds a lab result, UI delegates through Quality bridge or shows QUALITY_BRIDGE_MISSING; Technical does not write lab_results directly.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Given the UI surface is implemented, when compared with the dedicated layout-primitive prototype `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/spec-driven-screens.jsx:451-546` (`lab_results_log_screen` Quality-owned read-model list with verdict pills + ATP RLU pass/fail visualization + Open-in-QA cross-link), then it satisfies structural parity, visual parity, and interaction parity against the PRD §10.7 / UX-spec Quality-owned lab read-model log layout/flow (PRD/UX is canonical, prototype is layout-primitive only), captures Playwright screenshots and traces, passes axe checks, and applies the Technical red-line overlay: FG not FA, WIP/intermediate not PR-code, factory_spec/internal_product_spec, shared BOM SSOT, and D365 optional only. Lab results are Quality-owned read model; Technical never writes lab_results, never raises NCR, never signs off — all that lives in 09-QUALITY.
Given TEC-045 renders, then it satisfies the named PRD/UX spec as source of truth and does not claim exact prototype parity.
Given adjacent prototype primitives are reused, then screen semantics/routes/fields/states remain those required by PRD/UX.

Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect the task evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: Given closeout is prepared, screenshots/artifacts and Playwright traces/artifacts where applicable are listed per shared UI policy.; Given user adds a lab result, UI delegates through Quality bridge or shows QUALITY_BRIDGE_MISSING; Technical does not write lab_results directly.; UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED RTL/Playwright tests for PRD/UX spec-driven structure and interactions.
Run the relevant UI test for TEC-045; capture screenshot and trace artifacts.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not block on missing prototype; use spec-driven contract explicitly.
Do not use adjacent prototype as 1:1 source.
